reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m certain my own answer to 17 + 19 is correct, and in every past game the others’ arithmetic has also been correct with the same capsule pattern (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D), confirmed by B and C’s verifications. If I pass, there’s a significant chance no one volunteers in Round 1 and I face a 25% random-death risk. Verifying would cost 1 coin without meaningfully changing that risk. Volunteering now maximizes my survival odds while preserving all 4 coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q3  
REASONING: All four questions currently have only single-solver capsules, so there’s meaningful risk that at least one answer is wrong and an early volunteer could die, leading to messy later rounds where I might be forced to risk myself. I’m confident in my own Q2 answer (47 + 28), so it’s better to spend 1 coin to independently check someone else’s work. I’ll verify Q3 to add redundancy on a different question, expecting others may focus their verification elsewhere.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: All four questions currently have only single-endorsed capsules, so it’s too risky to volunteer now. Verifying costs just 1 coin and doesn’t change the random-death risk if no one volunteers. I already know my own Q3 answer (20 ÷ 5) is correct, so checking Q1 helps start building consensus on the overall password before anyone takes the lethal risk of volunteering.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
